{/*
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
@catalog-layout: accordion-group
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
CONTENT LAYOUT — set @catalog-layout above to one of:
  accordion-group  flat-table  grouped-tables  tree
Mixed layouts (e.g. summary table + AccordionGroup) are always valid.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

── ACCORDION GROUP (two-level, categorised) ─────────────────
Use when: items have well-defined top-level folder/category groupings
AND the catalog is long enough that a flat table would need scrolling.
Good for: components (by category), scripts (by directory).

TWO LEVELS:
  Level 1 — <AccordionGroup>  = one top-level folder or category
  Level 2 — <Accordion>       = every individual item inside that group

Optional: summary table BEFORE the AccordionGroups.
Include an "Unused" column (count = 0) so consumers can spot dead code.

| Category | Exports | Stable | Experimental | Deprecated | Broken | Placeholder | Unused |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Category Name] | N | N | N | N | N | N | N |
| **Total** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** | **N** |

Then one AccordionGroup per category/folder:

## [Category Name]

<AccordionGroup>

<Accordion title="[ComponentName] — [status]">
<ResponseField name="status" type="string">[stable | experimental | deprecated | broken | placeholder]</ResponseField>
<ResponseField name="description" type="string">[One-line description from JSDoc or source.]</ResponseField>
<ResponseField name="file" type="string">`/[path/to/file.jsx]`</ResponseField>
<ResponseField name="usage" type="number">[N pages (or: unused)]</ResponseField>
</Accordion>

<Accordion title="[ComponentName] — [status]">
...
</Accordion>

</AccordionGroup>

── AUDIT SECTION (always at bottom when non-empty) ──────────
Collects governance signals that need attention: orphaned components
(defined but not imported anywhere), broken items, etc.
Use a single <Accordion> — NOT wrapped in AccordionGroup.

<Accordion title="⚠️ Audit — [N items]">

| Component | Category | Status | Note |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [ComponentName] | `[category]` | `[status]` | [e.g. Not imported in any page] |

</Accordion>

── FLAT TABLE ───────────────────────────────────────────────
Use when: items are homogeneous, ungrouped, comparable side by side.
Good for: workflows, templates, issue types, small catalogs.

| Name | Description | Location | [Column] |
|---|---|---|---|
| [item] | [description] | `[path]` | [value] |

── GROUPED TABLES ───────────────────────────────────────────
Use when: items cluster into named groups but the catalog is short enough
that collapsing would hide more than it helps.
Each group gets a ## heading + its own table.

## [Group Name]

| Name | Description | [Column] | [Column] |
|---|---|---|---|
| [item] | [description] | [value] | [value] |

── TREE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Use when: the data IS a file/folder hierarchy.
Good for: page catalogs (v2/ tree), template catalogs (snippets/ tree).
Use defaultOpen on the top-level folder only.

<Tree>
  <Tree.Folder name="[root]" defaultOpen>
    <Tree.Folder name="[subfolder]">
      <Tree.File name="[file.mdx]" />
    </Tree.Folder>
    <Tree.File name="[file.mdx]" />
  </Tree.Folder>
</Tree>

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
*/}
